articles.title: Maya Jama's Transition from TV Host to Aspiring Actor
articles.summary: Maya Jama, a 31-year-old British television presenter known for hosting Love Island and appearing on The Masked Singer, is pursuing a career shift into acting. She reveals to the BBC that she has renewed ambition after earlier setbacks in the acting industry. In her youth, Jama auditioned for roles like in the series Skins but faced numerous rejections, which discouraged her. Instead, she built a successful presenting career, starting with YouTube videos and progressing to TV shows such as Cannonball and the MOBO Awards. Her big break came in 2022 when she took over as host of Love Island. Now, Jama is set to star in the second season of Guy Ritchie's Netflix series The Gentlemen, though her role is undisclosed. She expresses a desire to play characters vastly different from her public persona, specifically aiming for villainous or monstrous roles to defy expectations. Jama reflects on her journey, noting how persistence paid off in presenting and how she now feels doors have reopened for acting opportunities.

articles.title: Water-in-Diesel Emulsion: A Simple Solution to Cut Engine Pollution
articles.summary: Researchers from the Federal University of Technology Owerri in Nigeria have reviewed studies on Water-in-Diesel Emulsion (WiDE) technology, finding it can significantly reduce pollution from diesel engines. Diesel engines are widely used in transportation, agriculture, and industry due to their reliability and power, but they emit harmful pollutants like nitrogen oxides and particulate matter, contributing to smog, respiratory diseases, and climate change. Existing solutions like catalytic converters are effective but costly and complex. WiDE involves mixing small water droplets into diesel fuel using surfactants, which stabilize the blend. When burned, the water vaporizes, causing micro-explosions that improve fuel-air mixing, lower combustion temperatures, and reduce nitrogen oxide formation while decreasing soot emissions. Studies show WiDE can cut nitrogen oxides by up to 67% and particulate matter by up to 68%, with some reports of improved brake thermal efficiency, meaning better fuel use. The technology is practical and cost-effective as it doesn't require engine modifications, making it accessible for both developing and developed countries. Surfactants are crucial for stability, and future research should optimize their combinations and assess long-term engine effects. WiDE could complement other clean technologies to support broader environmental goals.

articles.title: Judge Halts Federal Reserve Probe Over Lack of Evidence
articles.summary: A U.S. judge has blocked the Department of Justice's investigation into the Federal Reserve, ruling that prosecutor Jeanine Pirro presented no evidence to justify the subpoenas for information. The judge, James Boasberg, stated the subpoenas were issued for an improper purpose to pressure Fed Chairman Jerome Powell into lowering interest rates or resigning. Powell had accused the Trump administration of using the legal probe as a pretext to influence monetary policy. Pirro, an ally of former President Donald Trump, plans to appeal the decision, arguing it undermines her ability to investigate potential crimes related to office renovation cost overruns. The case highlights concerns about political interference in the central bank and complicates White House efforts to appoint a new Fed chair. Some Republican senators support the judge's view, believing further investigation is unwarranted, while others threaten to block appointments until the matter is resolved. The Federal Reserve declined to comment, emphasizing the tension between judicial oversight and prosecutorial authority.

articles.title: Harry Styles Achieves Chart Success Amid Mixed Reviews
articles.summary: Harry Styles' latest album, Kiss All The Time. Disco Occasionally, has debuted at number one on the UK album charts, marking his third chart-topping album. According to the Official Charts Company, it sold over 183,000 copies in its first week, surpassing the 113,000 copies sold by his previous album, Harry's House, in 2022. A significant portion of sales—66,000 copies—were on vinyl, making it the year's biggest physical release. Despite this commercial success, the album received mixed reviews from critics. The Telegraph criticized it for lacking emotional depth, comparing it to a perfume advert, while The Guardian gave it three stars, calling it pleasant but occasionally good. In contrast, NME praised Styles for sounding liberated, and Clash magazine highlighted its personal and sensual evolution. Styles explained in interviews that the album was inspired by his post-tour experiences, where he aimed to be more open and engage with the world after his 22-month Love On Tour. He emphasized saying yes to new opportunities and connecting with people, which shaped the album's themes. Additionally, Styles launched the album with a concert in Manchester, which was filmed and released as a Netflix special.

articles.title: US Launches Trade Probe into Forced Labor in Multiple Countries
articles.summary: The United States has initiated a Section 301 trade investigation targeting 60 countries, including major economies like China, the European Union, Canada, and the UK. The probe, announced by US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer, aims to determine whether these countries are failing to block sales of goods produced with forced labor, thereby harming US businesses. Forced labor involves coercive work conditions that can lower production costs, giving foreign producers an artificial advantage. This move follows a recent Supreme Court ruling that struck down previous tariffs, prompting the Trump administration to seek new levies under different laws. The investigation could lead to tariffs being imposed, with public hearings scheduled for April. Additionally, the US has previously addressed forced labor concerns, such as through a 2022 law banning goods from China's Xinjiang region due to Uyghur minority issues. The process includes requirements for public notice and comment, aiming for a more orderly approach compared to past rapid tariff changes.

articles.title: From Russian Exile to Oscar Nominee: Pavel Talankin's Journey
articles.summary: Pavel Talankin, known as Pasha, was an events coordinator and videographer in Karabash, Russia, who fled into exile in 2024 after opposing President Putin's war efforts. Within two years, he transformed into an Oscar-nominated director for his documentary 'Mr Nobody Against Putin,' co-created with American director David Borenstein. The film, which uses humor to address the serious topic of authoritarianism and Russia's invasion of Ukraine, won the Bafta Film Awards. Pasha's story highlights his rise from obscurity to Hollywood recognition, including meetings with actors like Leonardo DiCaprio. His journey underscores the power of filmmaking as a form of resistance and coping mechanism under oppressive regimes, blending comedy with critical commentary on political realities.
